predictions.source_quote: Overall, we expect GDP growth of 4% over the course of 2022.
In both the United States and the euro area, we expect growth to slow down to 4%.
In the U.K., we expect growth of about 5.5%
in China we expect growth to fall to about 5%.
Across emerging markets, growth could prove uneven, aggregating to 5.5%.
bringing the Core Personal Consumption Expenditures Price Index for year-end 2022 in the range of 2.3%–2.6% year over year.
We expect headline CPI to peak between 4.5% and 5% in the first half of 2022
and approach 2.5% year over year by the end of 2022.
the official unemployment rate will reach the pre-pandemic low of 3.5% in mid-2022
In the United States, that balance should involve the Federal Reserve raising interest rates in the second half of 2022
Ultimately, we anticipate that the Federal Reserve will raise rates to at least 2.5% by the end of this cycle to keep wage pressures under control and to keep inflation expectations stable.

predictions.forecast_value: no rate hikes for at least 24 months
predictions.source_quote: we don't currently expect rate hikes over at least the next 24 months

predictions.variable: Bank of England first rate hike
predictions.forecast_value: December 2021 (or February 2022 if labor data disappoints)
predictions.source_quote: We expect the MPC to begin raising interest rates in December 2021, provided that October labor market data are in line with our expectations.
